new_terms.context: Refers to cooling systems that transfer heat from liquid to air, common in transitional cooling architectures.
Refers to 100% liquid cooling systems where heat is transferred between two liquid loops.
The primary water loop provided by the data center facility to remove heat from the TCS.
A cooling device that uses ambient air to cool a fluid without water consumption, often used in chiller-less designs.
A cooling tower that uses evaporation to pre-cool ambient air, increasing efficiency in high-density cooling.
A data center architecture that eliminates traditional chillers in favor of dry coolers or adiabatic systems.
Cooling infrastructure located within the IT equipment area (white space) of a data center.
A financial metric referring to the dollar value of specific components sold per megawatt of power capacity.
